challenges.name: Origins
challenges.keyTakeaway: FTP brute force and data exfiltration detection — analyzing plaintext FTP credentials and tracking unauthorized file transfers

challenges.name: Telly
challenges.keyTakeaway: Telnet plaintext exploitation — analyzing unencrypted remote access sessions and understanding legacy protocol risks

challenges.name: Noxious
challenges.keyTakeaway: LLMNR poisoning detection — extracting NTLMv2 hashes from network captures and understanding AD name resolution attacks

challenges.name: Reaper
challenges.keyTakeaway: NTLM relay attack detection — identifying LLMNR poisoning chains leading to relay attacks and lateral movement in Active Directory

challenges.name: Meerkat
challenges.keyTakeaway: HTTP traffic analysis — credential stuffing detection, CVE exploitation, and correlating Suricata alerts with PCAP evidence

challenges.name: Pikaptcha
challenges.keyTakeaway: ClickFix social engineering detection — identifying fake CAPTCHA pages that trick users into executing malicious PowerShell via Run dialog

challenges.name: Litter
challenges.keyTakeaway: DNS tunneling forensics — detecting dnscat2 C2 channels and decoding exfiltrated data from DNS queries

challenges.name: Compromised
challenges.keyTakeaway: Malware traffic analysis — Pikabot delivery via HTTP, self-signed TLS C2 channels, and DNS tunneling detection

challenges.name: Takedown
challenges.keyTakeaway: DarkGate malware delivery chain analysis — tracing multi-stage malware from VBScript dropper through PowerShell to LOLBin execution and C2

challenges.name: Packet Puzzle
challenges.keyTakeaway: CVE-2024-4577 PHP-CGI exploitation — detecting argument injection attacks against PHP servers and tracking post-exploitation privilege escalation

challenges.name: Knock Knock
challenges.keyTakeaway: Multi-phase attack reconstruction — port scanning, FTP password spray, port knocking, directory traversal, and ransomware deployment

challenges.name: Trent
challenges.keyTakeaway: Router command injection exploitation — analyzing CVE-2024-28353 against IoT/network devices, from initial compromise to reverse shell establishment
